Ad Text:

$130k incl super - $155k incl super

What’s on Offer

High-visibility roles with strong stakeholder engagement
Career progression across both advisory and assurance pathways
Supportive, inclusive culture with a focus on wellbeing and development

About the company
Our client is a leading International Retail organisation undergoing a major transformation to become more data-led. They are investing in a modern data and decision intelligence capability to drive smarter, faster commercial decisions across the entire business.

Drive enterprise-wide impact in a rare hybrid role combining data strategy and hands-on analytics. Reporting into a highly regarded leader with deep experience in both strategy and analytics, you will influence marketing, retail, and brand teams while defining how data is structured, accessed, and applied. Strong C-suite sponsorship ensures your work is visible and impactful.

Benefits

High-visibility role with autonomy and strong leadership support
Flexible hybrid working (3 days in Sydney office)
Exposure to modern data platforms, including Snowflake and emerging AI tools

Establish frameworks, standards, and best practices for analytics and data science

Consulting experience from a tier 1 or 1.5 firm is highly advantageous
Retail/ecommerce/FMCG experience is highly advantageous

Job Title: Head of Strategy, Architecture and Innovation (AI)
Ad Text:
You’ve led architecture before.

You’ve set standards. Influenced decisions. Helped shape platforms.

But in most environments, the real direction is already set.

The roadmap is locked. The big calls have been made. And architecture is brought in to support decisions, not define them.

You’re left working within constraints you didn’t create, shaping outcomes that were already heading in a certain direction.

And innovation?

It’s talked about. Pilots get run. But very little of it actually makes its way into how the platform operates at scale.

Why This Role Is Different

This role exists because the organisation has reached a genuine decision point.

The platform is stable. The environment is understood. But what comes next has not been fully defined yet.

The next phase of technology direction, what gets modernised, what gets retired, where investment goes, how new capability is introduced, is still open.

Architecture has historically operated close to delivery. The shift now is to elevate it to where it should be, setting direction at an enterprise level and shaping decisions before they are made.

At the same time, there is a deliberate move to introduce AI and emerging capability into the core platform in a way that is practical, governed and scalable.

You are stepping in before those decisions are locked in.

The Opportunity

You will work directly with the CIO to define the organisation’s long-term technology direction.

In practical terms, that means shaping a multi-year strategy, defining the enterprise target state, and guiding how major decisions are made across applications, data, integration and infrastructure.

On day one, you are walking into an environment where the questions are real:

What does the future platform actually look like?

What do we modernise versus retain?

Where does AI genuinely add value, and where does it not?

How do we move forward without over-engineering or over-complicating?

Your role is to bring clarity to those questions and help the organisation make better, more deliberate decisions.

This is not about producing strategy documents in isolation.

It is about influencing real outcomes, investment direction, platform shape, and long-term capability.

You will need the judgement to operate at executive level, and the depth to stay close enough to the detail to make strong calls when it matters.

The Environment and Context

The organisation operates a critical, high-value data and infrastructure platform within a regulated environment where trust, resilience and long-term stability matter.

It is well-funded, commercially disciplined, and supported by a Board and executive team who see technology as central to future performance.

Importantly, it is not weighed down by excessive scale or layers. There is room to move, space to shape, and genuine appetite to improve.

The architecture capability is established but still evolving, with clear scope to elevate its influence, maturity and role in shaping enterprise-wide decisions.

You are not stepping into a turnaround. You are stepping into a platform that works, but now needs to evolve with intent.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Co-develop and evolve the organisation’s long-term technology strategy and roadmap
Define the enterprise target state architecture across core platforms and services
Shift architecture from project-level solutioning to enterprise-wide direction setting
Guide major modernisation decisions, including legacy simplification and re-platforming
Shape how AI and emerging technologies are introduced, governed and scaled
Act as a trusted advisor to the CIO, Executive and Board on technology direction
Lead a small team and evolve the architecture capability as the function matures
Drive clearer, more commercially grounded technology decision-making

What You’ll Bring

You’ve operated at a senior architecture or strategy level in a complex environment.

You’ve moved beyond solution design into shaping direction, and you’re comfortable working where problems are not fully defined.

You know how to think at enterprise level, but you haven’t lost the ability to get into the detail when it matters. You’re confident challenging decisions, guiding trade-offs and helping organisations move forward with clarity.

You’ve likely worked in regulated or governance-heavy environments where decisions need to stand up commercially, operationally and technically.

You want to influence where things are going, not just support what’s already been decided.

Why Join

This is a role where your thinking will directly shape how the platform evolves over time.

You will work closely with an engaged CIO and executive team who want architecture to lead, not follow.

And you will operate in an environment where your decisions are visible, meaningful, and applied in real time.

For the right person, this is the kind of role that materially strengthens your trajectory and how you are seen in the market.

Next Steps

For a confidential discussion, please contact Steven Fulop at xceltium on 0418 994 446 or apply via SEEK.

Any direct or third-party applications will be filtered back to xceltium as the retained search partner on this search.

The Finance team’s goal is to be a trusted and collaborative partner to all the businesses and functional teams that we work with, bringing business acumen, financial expertise and insights to aid decision-making and deliver value to our stakeholders. We strive to generate value through providing specialist advice and making recommendations to optimize decision making, delivering transparent processes and implementing governance and controls to mitigate risk.

The Finance team’s goal is to be a trusted and collaborative partner to all the businesses and functional teams that we work with, bringing business acumen, financial expertise and insights to aid decision-making and deliver value to our stakeholders. We strive to generate value through providing specialist advice and making recommendations to optimize decision making, delivering transparent processes and implementing governance and controls to mitigate risk.

Let's Talk About the Team



ResMed is a global leader in connected health, with more than 8 million cloud-connected devices for daily remote patient monitoring. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for patients, prevent chronic disease progression, and lower healthcare costs.

The Product FP&A Team act as business advisors, steering strategic decisions that impact short, medium, and long-term financial performance with a global mindset. We challenge the organization to constantly innovate and avoid complacency, uncovering new waves of growth. By establishing and maintaining a data-driven culture, we push performance to aspirational levels, maximizing total shareholder returns but more importantly improving the lives of as many people as possible around the world.

Let's Talk About the Role



The Product Organization at ResMed is foundational to ResMed’s ability to innovate responsibly, scale globally, and meet the highest standards of safety, compliance, and product quality. 

In this role, you will own end-to-end cost center forecasting, reporting, and financial governance for all areas of Product and QA/RA, while specifically serving as a strategic thought partner to the Global Heads of Engineering and QA/RA. You will bring rigor, insight, and forward-looking analysis to complex investment decisions, ensuring resources are allocated in line with ResMed’s long-term growth and risk objectives.

This role requires strong FP&A fundamentals, executive-level communication skills, and the ability to influence senior technical and regulatory leaders in a highly matrixed, global environment.

Key Responsibilities



Financial Planning, Forecasting & Reporting

Own the annual operating plan, quarterly forecasts, and long-range outlook for all Product and Quality & Regulatory cost centers.

Develop and maintain detailed headcount, opex, and capex forecasts, ensuring accuracy, transparency, and clear linkage to business priorities and cross functional initiatives.

Lead monthly close, variance analysis, and management reporting, providing clear explanations of performance vs. plan and outlook.

Establish and maintain robust financial controls and governance, ensuring adherence to budgets, investment approval processes, and corporate policies.

Business Partnership & Decision Support

Act as a trusted finance partner to the Global Head of Engineering and Global Head of Quality & Regulatory, supporting strategic and operational decision-making.

Provide financial insight on organizational design, workforce planning, productivity initiatives, and investment trade-offs.

Translate complex technical and regulatory initiatives into clear financial narratives for senior leadership.

Challenge assumptions constructively, balancing innovation velocity with financial discipline and risk management.

Strategic & Long-Range Planning

Support long-range planning by linking functional investment plans to ResMed’s broader growth strategy and product roadmap.

Assess return on investment, capacity planning, and cost efficiency for major initiatives, platforms, and capabilities.

Partner cross-functionally with Portfolio Management, Project Management Operations, and Corporate Finance to ensure alignment across planning horizons.

Process, Systems & Continuous Improvement

Drive continuous improvement in FP&A processes, models, and reporting, leveraging automation and analytics where possible.

Improve forecast accuracy and insight generation through better data, tools, and standardized operating rhythms.

Translate business needs into scalable financial processes and system requirements.

Let’s Talk About You


7+ years of progressive experience in FP&A, finance, or related roles, with strong cost center management and forecasting expertise.

Proven experience partnering with senior functional leaders, ideally in Engineering, R&D, Quality, Regulatory, or other technical organizations.

Strong financial modeling and analytical skills, with the ability to distill complexity into actionable insights.

Deep understanding of headcount-driven cost structures, investment governance, and operating expense management.

Executive-ready communication skills, including the ability to influence without authority.

Experience operating in a fast-paced, global, matrixed environment.

Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field; MBA or CPA/CFA a plus.

Job Title: Principal Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) Analyst
Ad Text:
Company description:



Sandfire continues to make strong progress toward its goal of being a sustainable mining company and global copper producer of significance and is listed on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX: SFR).

Headquartered in Perth, Western Australia, Sandfire has a demonstrated commitment to the highest safety standards. Our strategy is focused on both near-mine and regional opportunities as we seek to leverage our strategic position in the Iberian Pyrite Belt in Spain and the Kalahari Copper Belt in Botswana and materially increase the life of our modern processing hubs.

Sandfire Resources has entered into an exciting partnership with Havilah Resources to advance the Kalkaroo Copper-Gold Project in South Australia.



Job description:



Due to growth, we are currently seeking a Principal Finance Planning & Analysis (FP&A) Analyst person to join our team of talented professionals. This role reports to the Group FP&A manager and will be part of the broader Finance function, interfacing with global asset-based FP&A teams. The role is responsible for leading the accurate and timely delivery of group budgeting and quarterly forecast, preparing analysis and insights to support internal decision-making and market reporting obligations, preparing the group's performance reports and driving continuous improvement to systems, processes and broader FP&A capabilities across the group.

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

Act as FP&A subject matter expert across Sandfire group, with a focus on improving governance over existing financial processes and systems.
Group FP&A focal point on system enhancements including opportunity identification, preparing proof of concepts and needs analysis, prioritisation of work programs, user acceptance testing, user training, change management and documentation, stakeholder management, amongst other things.
Manage various stakeholders across the group to ensure accurate and timely delivery of information.
Preparation of budgets, quarterly forecasts, group consolidation of financial plans including the analysis of financial information and production physicals.
Preparation of presentation of planning outputs to support Executive decision-making and approvals.
Maintain and communicate planning timetables in accordance with the Integrated Planning Group Standard and coordination of deliverables by the various business areas.
Preparation of Board papers to support approval of the budget.
Preparation of group performance reporting providing analytical insights to the group's performance against budgets and forecasts.
Preparation of annual and half year reporting deliverables including the operating and financial review sections of these reports.
Provide analysis to support quarterly market reporting, investor updates or other external disclosures.
Maintain a high degree of data integrity across systems, presentations and reports ensuring data is reconciled to source systems and any anomalies are identified and resolved in a timely manner.
Support the transformation of the FP&A function including the design, development and implementation of systems, reports, processes and analysis, as well as support the transition of intra-function responsibilities.
Provide business partnering support to corporate functions and operational stakeholders.
Act as FP&A subject matter expert across Sandfire group, with a focus on improving governance over existing financial processes and systems.
Group FP&A focal point on system enhancements including opportunity identification, preparing proof of concepts and needs analysis, prioritisation of work programs, user acceptance testing, user training, change management and documentation, stakeholder management, amongst other things.

About you

Commerce/Business undergraduate and CA/CPA post graduate qualifications or a technical qualification.
10+ years of experience in FP&A/management accounting/business partnering within the mining sector in a multi-asset company preferably within an ASX listed mining company
System experience with major ERPs, reporting and planning systems (Anaplan, Onestream and Pronto highly desirable).
Process oriented with a mindset of developing and maintaining sustainable processes.
Ability to work at pace and to deliver against deadlines without rework.
Development and/or management of complex financial models.
Ability to work with global stakeholders from different cultural backgrounds.
Solid understanding of accounting principles, financial statements and production and sales measures applicable to the mining industry.
Excellent analytical and numerical skills, with the proven ability to synthesise large data sets with a high attention to detail.
Strong written and oral communication, including the ability to distil key messages to different audiences (finance and non-finance stakeholders.
Project and time management across multiple tasks (challenging, complex and dynamic deliverables and tight timelines).
Strong understanding of the mining lifecycle and value chain.
Autonomous and self-starter that can work independently.
Good leadership qualities to actively mentor and support team members and other stakeholders.



We offer:



At Sandfire we value our people and offer a range of benefits and personal development opportunities including:

Attractive, market-competitive remuneration package
Flexible work arrangements
Salary continuance insurance scheme.
Corporate health insurance discount.
Ongoing professional development opportunities

If you possess the above experience and would like to be part of a values-driven, inclusive, and respectful organisation, please apply now by clicking on the apply now button

below.

Sandfire is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace for all employees.

Kindly note that agency applications will not be accepted for this position.

Lab3 is one of Australasia’s largest Azure engineering practices, specialising in Cloud & DevOps, Data, IoT & AI, Modern Workplace, Security, and Networks. We’re seeking a Principal Consultant- AI  to join our Professional Services team.  This is a senior, client-facing leadership role accountable for shaping, selling, and delivering high impact AI and Agentic AI programs for enterprise clients, working across the AI lifecycle from prototyping to production.

 

The Role:

Client Leadership & Advisory 

Act as a trusted advisor to senior client stakeholders
Lead AI strategy engagements, including use case prioritization, value articulation, and delivery roadmaps. 
Translate business strategy into scalable AI architectures and operating models. 

Solution Architecture & Technical Authority 

Provide architectural leadership across Agentic AI, GenAI, RAG, MLOps, and data platforms on Azure. 
Define reference architectures, nonfunctional requirements, and design patterns for enterprise AI systems. 
Review and approve HLD/LLD produced by delivery teams; intervene on complex or high-risk designs. 
Guide model selection, orchestration patterns, governance approaches, and platform choices aligned to business constraints. 

Delivery Oversight & Risk Management 

Own end to end technical accountability for large or complex AI programs. 
Derisk delivery through early architecture validation, proof points, and phased adoption strategies. 
Establish delivery standards across security, observability, MLOps, and responsible AI. 
Support engagement leads with estimates, delivery plans, and dependency management. 

PreSales & Commercial Contribution 

Lead or support pre-sales activities including discovery, solution shaping, estimates, SOW input, and technical assurance. 
Contribute to win-strategy through differentiated architecture, accelerators, and compelling narratives. 
Act as a technical escalation point during bids and client negotiations. 

Practice & Capability Development 

Define and evolve AI standards, accelerators, and reusable assets for LAB3. 
Mentor Senior Engineers and Consultants; uplift architectural thinking and consulting maturity. 
Contribute to hiring, role design, and skills benchmarking. 
Represent LAB3 externally through thought leadership, client showcases, and partner engagements. 

Governance, Security & Responsible AI 

Set best practice approaches for AI governance, risk, compliance, and ethics. 
Ensure solutions align with enterprise security models
Champion responsible AI principles

 

Our ideal candidate:

 

Core Technical Expertise:

Microsoft AI & Data Stack: Azure AI Foundry, Azure AI Search (vector & hybrid), Azure OpenAI, Azure ML, Fabric / Databricks. 
Agentic AI & GenAI: Agent frameworks, RAG at scale, orchestration patterns, tool/function calling, prompt optimisation. 
Architecture: Distributed systems, event driven design, API strategies, data platform integration. 
MLops & Platform Engineering: CI/CD, model lifecycle management, IaC, containers, AKS. 
Observability: End to end telemetry across application, data, and model layers. 
Security: Enterprise identity, networking, data protection, and compliance patterns. 

Consulting & Leadership Capability 

Proven experience leading large or complex client engagements. 
Strong commercial acumen: value framing, estimation, and scope control. 
Exceptional communication—credible with executives and deeply technical teams alike. 
Ability to coach, influence, and set direction without direct authority. 

Must be based anywhere in Australia, a citizen, and ideally hold security clearance

What’s in it for you?  

Be part of a team that leverages modern technologies to solve real problems and provides top level of customer satisfaction
Work with a Microsoft Partner of the Year award winner with multiple specialisations
Be supported by experienced peers and leaders that value technical expertise and encourages innovation, with clear career pathways and ongoing learning
Enjoy a supportive workplace that promotes inclusion, flexibility, diversity, and celebrates differences.
Take advantage of largely working from home in our remote/hybrid workplace and enjoy the flexibility to balance your life  
 Thrive in a community with strong values #BeTrue #TeamUp #StandOut #ThinkAhead #FearLessAchieveMore 

   

Apply now, call or connect on LinkedIn for further information.
